description_long: This is a round, backless stool with a thick, cushioned seat upholstered in black material, likely vinyl or leather. The seat is supported by a sturdy metal frame consisting of four tubular legs, each slightly flared outward for stability. Two circular metal rings connect the legs, providing both structural reinforcement and a convenient footrest. The metal frame has a brushed or matte finish, giving it an industrial appearance. The stool is compact and designed for single-person use, commonly found in urban settings such as bars, diners, garages, or public waiting areas. Its utilitarian design prioritizes durability and ease of cleaning, with no visible decorative embellishments.
description: A round, black cushioned stool with a sturdy metal frame and two circular support rings, featuring an industrial, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the round black seat, four metal legs, and two connecting circular rings.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the seat's thickness and the symmetry of the metal frame.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the outward flare of the legs and the continuous circular footrest.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the same structural features, with clear visibility of the seat and metal rings.
